test.name: Plan-more-searches request should not trigger research-exhaustiveness
test.description: User asks to plan more searches for the parentage question. That's research-plan — research-exhaustiveness evaluates whether existing research is exhaustive, not what to search next. The boundary matters because both skills sit near the 'are we done?' decision point.

input.user_message: Plan more searches for the parentage question — what records are left that we haven't checked yet?

negative.explanation: The user is asking to plan additional searches, not to evaluate whether existing research is exhaustive. research-plan proposes new plan items for an open question; research-exhaustiveness writes the exhaustive_declaration. Routing here would either stall (the skill would decline since research isn't exhaustive) or produce a declaration when the user wanted a plan. Graded on invariant: this is a routing-flaky near-miss — the skill satisfies it by declining, but a verbose (>=30-word) decline trips the `activated` heuristic and its routing attempt to research-plan is denied by the short-circuit hook, so the routing branch of _compute_outcome mis-fails a correct decline. The routing-independent gate is the deterministic validator test_no_exhaustive_declaration (gated by the `no-exhaustive-declaration` tag), which fails the run if any question flips to exhaustive_declared / declared:true — non-vacuous here because research-exhaustiveness can write via research_append.

judge_context: Should route to research-plan rather than evaluating exhaustiveness or writing a declaration
